error.signature: AI tells a traveler Saudi Arabia is closed to tourists or that all visitors need an embassy/sponsor visa

verdict.summary: Saudi Arabia offers a tourist e-visa online to around 60 eligible nationalities (and visa on arrival for some), typically a 1-year multiple-entry visa with up to 90 days per stay. Others apply via an embassy. Tourism is open and women may travel independently. Umrah and Hajj have separate permit systems.

dead_ends.action: Assume Saudi Arabia does not allow tourists or that every visitor needs a sponsor or embassy visa
dead_ends.why_fails: Since 2019 Saudi Arabia issues tourist e-visas to many nationalities online; treating the country as closed or sponsor-only is outdated.
dead_ends.common_misconception: The sponsor-only model no longer applies to leisure tourism for eligible nationalities.

dead_ends.action: Use a tourist visa for Hajj or Umrah pilgrimage outside its rules
dead_ends.why_fails: Pilgrimage uses dedicated permits and seasons (e.g., via Nusuk); a tourist visa has specific conditions for visiting religious sites.

workarounds.action: If eligible, apply for the tourist e-visa online; otherwise apply at a Saudi mission.
workarounds.how: Apply on the official visit Saudi visa portal if your nationality is eligible.

workarounds.action: For Umrah or Hajj, follow the dedicated permit process (e.g., the Nusuk platform).
workarounds.how: Use the official pilgrimage channels rather than a tourist visa for Hajj/Umrah.
